questions.prompt: What do the three Ad Relevance Diagnostics rankings tell you?

choices.text: Quality ranking, engagement rate ranking, and conversion rate ranking each show how your ad compares to others competing for the same audience — Below Average, Average, or Above Average. Below Average dimensions reduce effective bid substantially in the auction. The diagnostic explicitly tells you which dimension is weak: low quality = creative or post-click issues; low engagement = hook/format problems; low conversion = LP or pixel issues. Read it, address the specific issue, and total value goes up.

questions.explanation: Three rankings = quality, engagement, conversion. Below Average = lever to fix.

questions.prompt: An ad has 'Below Average' conversion rate ranking. What's the diagnostic process?

choices.text: Check the post-click experience. Conversion rate ranking compares your click-to-conversion rate vs others targeting the same audience. Below Average means clicks aren't converting. Check: landing page speed (slow LP = bounces), mobile-friendliness, promise-fulfillment (does the LP deliver what the ad promised?), conversion path complexity, pixel/CAPI accuracy (bad data can show as low conversion rate). Fix the weakest link. Also verify the right conversion event is being tracked — if Meta is optimizing for purchases but the pixel fires for any page view, the system has wrong signal. Bid changes alone don't fix conversion rate problems.

questions.explanation: Conversion rate ranking = post-click issue. Fix LP speed, mobile, promise-fulfillment, pixel accuracy.
